title: Undiscovered Gems In Canada To Explore This April 2026
content:
As Canadian retail sales show a modest gain, largely driven by increased gas spending, and central banks maintain steady interest rates amid global uncertainties, the market is cautiously optimistic about economic fundamentals and potential earnings growth. In this environment, identifying undiscovered gems requires a focus on companies poised to benefit from resilient sectors and adaptable strategies that align with evolving market dynamics.

Let's explore several standout options from the results in the screener.

Calfrac Well Services

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Calfrac Well Services Ltd. offers specialized oilfield services across Canada, the United States, and Argentina, with a market capitalization of CA$571.38 million.

Operations: Calfrac generates revenue primarily from its oil well equipment and services segment, totaling CA$1.39 billion. The company's market capitalization stands at CA$571.38 million.

Calfrac Well Services, a relatively small player in the energy services sector, has demonstrated substantial earnings growth of 391.3% over the past year, outpacing industry averages. The company's net debt to equity ratio stands at a satisfactory 29.6%, reflecting prudent financial management as it reduced from 79.1% over five years. Despite trading at nearly 80% below its estimated fair value, Calfrac's EBIT covers interest payments only 2.8 times, indicating potential risk in debt servicing capability. Recent executive changes with Scarlett Crockatt as CFO and Tyler Dahlseide as CEO aim to optimize leadership and operational efficiency amidst these dynamics.

Calfrac Well Services aims to enhance revenue and margins through fleet modernization and expansion in Argentina. Click here to explore the full narrative on Calfrac's strategic initiatives and growth potential.TSX:CFW Earnings and Revenue Growth as at Apr 2026

North West

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: The North West Company Inc. operates as a retailer of food and everyday products and services across northern Canada, rural Alaska, the South Pacific, and the Caribbean with a market capitalization of CA$2.45 billion.

Story Continues

Operations: North West generates revenue primarily from the retail of food and everyday products. The company reported a gross profit margin of 31.5%, indicating its efficiency in managing production costs relative to sales revenue.

North West, a smaller player in the retail sector, has shown resilience with its debt management and profitability. Over the past five years, it reduced its debt to equity ratio from 55.7% to 37.8%, reflecting improved financial health. The company's interest payments are well-covered by EBIT at 12.6 times coverage, indicating strong earnings relative to debt obligations. Trading at 64% below estimated fair value suggests potential undervaluation in the market's eyes. Despite a modest earnings growth of 1.6% last year compared to the industry's average of 3.6%, North West maintains high-quality earnings and positive free cash flow generation, underscoring its operational efficiency and financial stability amidst competitive pressures.

Click here to discover the nuances of North West with our detailed analytical health report. Explore historical data to track North West's performance over time in our Past section.TSX:NWC Earnings and Revenue Growth as at Apr 2026

Heliostar Metals

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Heliostar Metals Ltd. is involved in gold and silver exploration and mining operations in the United States and Mexico, with a market cap of CA$662.93 million.

Operations: Heliostar Metals Ltd. does not currently report revenue from its gold and silver exploration and mining activities in the United States and Mexico.

Heliostar Metals, a Canadian junior mining company, is making strides with its Ana Paula project in Mexico, where recent drilling has revealed promising results. The company reported 28.95 meters grading 11.24 g/t gold and another interval of 69.15 meters at 10.09 g/t gold from the High Grade Panel, highlighting its potential for high-grade mineralization. Trading at a substantial discount to fair value estimates and free of debt, Heliostar appears financially sound despite shareholder dilution last year. With revenue projected to grow by over 42% annually, Heliostar's exploration efforts seem poised for continued success in the metals industry.

title: Uncovering Canada's Hidden Stock Gems This April 2026
content:
In April 2026, the Canadian market is navigating through a complex economic landscape, with inflationary pressures expected to peak before moderating later in the year. Despite these challenges, the S&P/TSX index has shown resilience with a notable recovery from its March lows. In this environment, identifying hidden stock gems requires a keen understanding of how companies are positioned to weather temporary price shocks and leverage any resulting opportunities for growth.

We'll examine a selection from our screener results.

Stack Capital Group

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Stack Capital Group Inc. is a public investment firm focused on private equity and venture capital across various stages of company development, with a market cap of CA$350.68 million.

Operations: Stack Capital Group generates revenue primarily through its unclassified services, amounting to CA$56.71 million.

Stack Capital Group, a nimble player in the Canadian market, has demonstrated impressive earnings growth of 139.8% over the past year, outpacing the Capital Markets industry’s -8.7%. Despite a lower net profit margin of 67.6% compared to last year, its price-to-earnings ratio stands attractively at 9.3x against the broader Canadian market's 16.9x. The company remains debt-free for five years and boasts high-quality earnings but faces challenges with negative free cash flow and shareholder dilution recently noted through private placements raising CAD 31 million in April 2026.

Dive into the specifics of Stack Capital Group here with our thorough health report. Evaluate Stack Capital Group's historical performance by accessing our past performance report.TSX:STCK Debt to Equity as at Apr 2026

Total Energy Services

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Total Energy Services Inc. is an energy services company operating in Canada, the United States, Australia, and internationally with a market cap of CA$824.55 million.

Operations: Total Energy Services generates revenue primarily from its Compression and Process Services segment (CA$526.94 million), followed by Contract Drilling Services (CA$332.69 million), Well Servicing (CA$125.29 million), and Rentals and Transportation Services (CA$79.82 million).

Story Continues

Total Energy Services seems to be carving a niche with its strategic investments and geographic spread. The company reported a notable earnings growth of 22.1% last year, outpacing the industry average of 7.1%. Recent figures show annual sales reaching CAD 1.06 billion, up from CAD 906.78 million the prior year, while net income rose to CAD 74.22 million from CAD 60.8 million. With debt levels reduced significantly over five years—from a ratio of 45.6% to just 9.1%—the firm appears financially sound, further underscored by its substantial share buyback program amounting to CA$28.67 million recently completed under an earlier announcement.

Total Energy Services is capitalizing on LNG export growth with strategic capacity expansion. Click here to explore the full narrative on Total Energy Services.TSX:TOT Debt to Equity as at Apr 2026

TWC Enterprises

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: TWC Enterprises Limited owns, operates, and manages golf clubs under the ClubLink One Membership More Golf brand in Canada and the United States, with a market capitalization of CA$543.40 million.

Operations: TWC Enterprises generates revenue primarily from its Canadian Golf Club Operations, which contribute CA$178.55 million, and its US Golf Club Operations, adding CA$25.58 million.

TWC Enterprises, a notable player in Canada's hospitality sector, showcases intriguing financial dynamics. The company has significantly reduced its debt-to-equity ratio from 28.5% to 5.2% over five years, reflecting a stronger balance sheet position. Despite a slight annual earnings decline of 0.4% over the past five years, recent performance is robust with a 40.8% earnings growth last year surpassing industry averages of 32.3%. TWC's price-to-earnings ratio stands at an appealing 10x against the broader Canadian market's 16.9x, suggesting potential value for investors seeking opportunities in this space while maintaining high-quality earnings and positive free cash flow status.

Dundee

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Dundee Corporation focuses on acquiring mineral resource assets and has a market capitalization of CA$353.62 million.

Operations: The company has a market capitalization of CA$353.62 million, but there are no specific revenue segments detailed in the available data.

Dundee Corporation, a player in the Canadian market, has seen its net income jump from CA$59.11 million to CA$320.5 million over the past year, with basic earnings per share rising to CA$3.57 from CA$0.64. Operations: Magellan Aerospace generates revenue from engineering and manufacturing aeroengine and aerostructure components for aerospace markets across Canada, the U.S., and Europe. The company's financial performance is influenced by its cost structure, which includes expenses related to production and operations.

Story Continues

Magellan Aerospace, a Canadian player in the aerospace sector, has been showcasing solid financial health. Trading at 38.1% below its estimated fair value, it offers potential for value seekers. Over the past five years, earnings have grown impressively at 64.2% annually, though recent growth of 11.1% lagged behind industry peers at 22.7%. The company's debt to equity ratio improved from 9.3 to 6.3 over five years, reflecting prudent financial management with interest payments well-covered by EBIT (41x). Recent strategic alliances and a steady dividend policy further highlight Magellan's commitment to growth and shareholder returns in a competitive market landscape.

Sierra Madre Gold and Silver, a promising player in the mining sector, has seen its debt to equity ratio rise from 3.9% to 13.6% over five years while maintaining more cash than total debt. The company's recent profitability and projected earnings growth of 44% annually highlight its potential, though interest payments remain poorly covered with an EBIT coverage of just 2.1 times. title: Mako Mining Receives Approval to List on NASDAQ
content:
VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / March 26, 2026 / Mako Mining Corp. (TSX-V:MKO)(OTCQX:MAKOF) ("Mako" or the "Company") is pleased to announce it has received approval to list its common shares (the "Shares") on The Nasdaq Capital Market LLC ("NASDAQ"). Trading is expected to commence on NASDAQ at the open of trading on March 30, 2026, under the symbol "MAKO". Concurrently with the listing, the Shares will cease to be quoted on the OTCQX® Best Market but will continue to trade on the TSX Venture Exchange under the existing symbol "MKO".

Akiba Leisman, CEO of Mako states, "Joining the NASDAQ marks a significant milestone for Mako. The listing will both elevate our profile in the United States and provide greater visibility and exposure to a broader institutional and retail investor base. We expect additional increases in our trading liquidity, which enhances the Company's ability to be index eligible, and provides for a stronger currency when looking at additional acquisition opportunities."

Existing shareholders will be able to trade the Shares on NASDAQ following completion of the listing. The Company's listing on NASDAQ remains subject to the declaration of effectiveness of the Company's Form 40-F Registration Statement registering the Shares with the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC").

About Mako

Mako Mining Corp. is a publicly listed gold mining, development and exploration company. The Company operates the high-grade San Albino gold mine in Nueva Segovia, Nicaragua, which ranks as one of the highest-grade open pit gold mines globally and offers district-scale exploration potential. Mako also owns two assets in the US: the Moss Mine in Arizona, an open pit gold mine in northwestern Arizona and the Mt. Hamilton Project, a fully permitted heap leach project in Nevada. Mako also holds a 100% interest in the PEA-stage Eagle Mountain Project in Guyana, South America. Eagle Mountain is the subject of engineering, environmental and mine permitting activity.

For further information about Mako, please contact Akiba Leisman, Chief Executive Officer, at (917) 558-5289 or aleisman@makominingcorp.com, or visit our website at www.makominingcorp.com and our profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

Neither the TSXV nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

Forward-Looking Information: Certain statements herein contain forward-looking statements and forward-looking information within the meaning of applicable securities laws. Such forward-looking statements or information include but are not limited to statements or information with respect to the business plans of the Company, the continued listing the TSX Venture Exchange, the timing for trading on NASDAQ and the effectiveness of the Form 40-F Registration Statement. Forward-looking statements or information often can be identified by the use of words such as "estimate", "project", "believe", "anticipate", "intend", "expect", "plan," "may," or "should" and the variations or comparable terminology of these words are intended to identify forward-looking statements and information.

Story Continues

The Company has made numerous assumptions including among other things, assumptions about general business and economic conditions, and the gold mining, development and exploration industry generally. The foregoing list of assumptions is not exhaustive.

Although management of the Company believes that the assumptions made and the expectations represented by such statements or information are reasonable, there can be no assurance that forward-looking statements or information herein will prove to be accurate. Forward-looking statements and information are based on assumptions and involve known and unknown risks which may cause actual results to be materially different from any future results, expressed or implied, by such forward-looking statements or information. These factors include, without limitation, risks relating to: the Nasdaq listing not being completed; the SEC not declaring the Form 40-F effective, the expectation that trading liquidity will increase; the enhancement of the Company's ability to be index eligible; the provision of a stronger currency when looking at additional acquisition opportunities; the availability of financing for the Company; business and economic conditions in the gold mining, development and exploration industry generally; the speculative nature of the Company's research and development programs; unanticipated events related to regulatory and licensing matters and environmental matters; changes in general economic conditions or conditions in the financial markets; changes in laws; and other risks and uncertainties as disclosed in the Company's public disclosure filings on EDGAR at sec.gov/edgar and SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. The forward-looking information and forward-looking statements contained in this news release are made as of the date of this news release, and the Company does not undertake to update any forward-looking information or forward-looking statements, except in accordance with applicable securities laws.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

SOURCE: Mako Mining Corp.

title: We Did The Math RWJ Can Go To $54
content:
Looking at the underlying holdings of the ETFs in our coverage universe at ETF Channel, we have compared the trading price of each holding against the average analyst 12-month forward target price, and computed the weighted average implied analyst target price for the ETF itself. For the Invesco S&P SmallCap 600 Revenue ETF (Symbol: RWJ), we found that the implied analyst target price for the ETF based upon its underlying holdings is $53.77 per unit. With RWJ trading at a recent price near $48.03 per unit, that means that analysts see 11.94% upside for this ETF looking through to the average analyst targets of the underlying holdings. Three of RWJ's underlying holdings with notable upside to their analyst target prices are Quanex Building Products Corp (Symbol: NX), Xencor, Inc (Symbol: XNCR), and Dynavax Technologies Corp (Symbol: DVAX). Although NX has traded at a recent price of $13.81/share, the average analyst target is 163.09% higher at $36.33/share. Similarly, XNCR has 130.18% upside from the recent share price of $10.47 if the average analyst target price of $24.10/share is reached, and analysts on average are expecting DVAX to reach a target price of $22.67/share, which is 127.57% above the recent price of $9.96. Below is a twelve month price history chart comparing the stock performance of NX, XNCR, and DVAX: Below is a summary table of the current analyst target prices discussed above: NameSymbolRecent PriceAvg. Analyst 12-Mo. Target% Upside to Target Invesco S&P SmallCap 600 Revenue ETFRWJ$48.03$53.7711.94% Quanex Building Products CorpNX$13.81$36.33163.09% Xencor, IncXNCR$10.47$24.10130.18% Dynavax Technologies CorpDVAX$9.96$22.67127.57% Are analysts justified in these targets, or overly optimistic about where these stocks will be trading 12 months from now? Do the analysts have a valid justification for their targets, or are they behind the curve on recent company and industry developments? A high price target relative to a stock's trading price can reflect optimism about the future, but can also be a precursor to target price downgrades if the targets were a relic of the past. These are questions that require further investor research. 10 ETFs With Most Upside To Analyst Targets » Also see:  Dividend Bargains You Can Buy Cheaper Than Insiders Did  Funds Holding KOG  MAKO Historical Stock Prices

The views and opinions expressed herein are the views and opinions of the author and do not necessarily reflect those of Nasdaq, Inc.
